subject: Chemistry
question:
It is because of inability of ns2 electrons of the valence shell to participate in bonding that
(1) Sn2+ and Pb2+ are both oxidising and reducing
(2) Sn4+ is reducing while Pb4+ is oxidising
(3) Sn2+ is reducing while Pb4+ is oxidising
(4) Sn2+ is oxidising while Pb4+ is reducing
$Answer (3)$
Sol. Inability of ns2 electrons of the valence shell to participate in bonding on moving down the group in heavier p-block elements is called inert pair effect.
As a result, Pb(II) is more stable than Pb(IV)
Sn(IV) is more stable than Sn(II)
Therefore, Pb(IV) is easily reduced to Pb(II)
Therefore, Pb(IV) is oxidising agent
Sn(II) is easily oxidised to Sn(IV)
Therefore, Sn(II) is reducing agent

option.A: Sn2+ and Pb2+ are both oxidising and reducing
option.B: Sn4+ is reducing while Pb4+ is oxidising
option.C: Sn2+ is reducing while Pb4+ is oxidising
option.D: Sn2+ is oxidising while Pb4+ is reducing

answer: Sn2+ is reducing while Pb4+ is oxidising
explanation:
Inability of ns2 electrons of the valence shell to participate in bonding on moving down the group in heavier p-block elements is called inert pair effect.
As a result, Pb(II) is more stable than Pb(IV)
Sn(IV) is more stable than Sn(II)
Therefore, Pb(IV) is easily reduced to Pb(II)
Therefore, Pb(IV) is oxidising agent
Sn(II) is easily oxidised to Sn(IV)
Therefore, Sn(II) is reducing agent
